title: DPC-seq: Genome-wide mapping of DNA-protein crosslinks (DPC) induced by endogenous aldehydes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"lu2yt","text":"DNA-protein crosslinks (DPC) induced by endogenous aldehydes are highly toxic DNA lesions that interfere with replication and transcription. To investigate how cells overcome DPC lesions and to understand the cellular toxicity of aldehydes and their impact as DPC in living organisms, we established a high-throughput sequencing method to explore the genome-wide distribution of DPC (DPC-seq). This protocol enables the measurement of DPC distribution and repair kinetics using cells obtained from living animal organs or tissue culture samples treated with formaldehyde.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"h2o4x","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Follow the provided protocol or your original one to prepare homogenised cell samples from animal organs (e.g. bone marrow) or tissue culture treated with aldehydes (e.g., 0.6 mM formaldehyde for 1 h in our paper).
Lyse the cells with SDS buffer (2% SDS; 20 mM Tris, pH 7.5) and store them at -80°C.
Sonicate the sample using a sonicator (Covaris: 5% duty factor, 200 cycles per burst, 16 min at 25°C).
Add equal volume of KCl buffer (200 mM KCl; 20 mM Tris, pH 7.5).
Centrifuge the sample for 5 min at 20,000 g at 4°C.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 750 μL KCl buffer.
Incubate the sample at 55℃ for 5 min, followed by on ice for 1 min.
Centrifuge the sample for 5 min at 20,000 g at 4°C.
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 500 μL KCl buffer with 0.04 mg/ml Proteinase K.
Incubate the sample with gentle shake at 500 rpm for 3 h at 55°C, followed by on ice for 1 min.
Centrifuge the sample for 5 min at 20,000 g at 4°C and collect the supernatant to purify DNA using a QIAGEN PCR purification column.
Next-generation sequencing libraries are prepared using NEB Next Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina or MGIEasy Universal DNA Library Prep Set, according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
creator: Protocols Importer
